mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating unemployment signals deteriorating labor demand and impending economic contraction, triggering recession fears that compress equity multiples and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment acceleration signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real GDP and housing starts indicates broad-based demand expansion, supporting corporate earnings revisions and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.02 when both series rise concurrently, or forward returns are negative during confirmed demand expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects supply chain normalization and capacity utilization, boosting near-term equity returns by confirming stable output growth and reducing supply-side inflation pressures.
failure_condition: INDPRO rises but SPX falls due to stagflation, or the signal shows no predictive power over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contraction in retail sales indicates weakening consumer demand, which directly reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX rallies despite retail sales drops, or the signal fails to predict returns when consumer credit conditions remain loose.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation combined with restrictive monetary policy creates a profit margin squeeze and valuation compression, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as real rates rise.
failure_condition: Equity multiples expand despite high inflation and tight policy, or the interaction term shows zero correlation with forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month payroll growth signals economic resilience and supports risk appetite, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as institutional flows increase.
failure_condition: Payroll growth correlates negatively with SPX returns, or the signal produces zero alpha after transaction costs over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Housing permit declines act as a leading indicator for economic slowdown, predicting negative S&P 500 forward returns specifically when the business cycle peaks and monetary policy remains restrictive.
failure_condition: SPX rises despite low permits, or the threshold signal fails to predict downturns during structural housing corrections.
